page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.2xlarge is a burstable instance belonging to the ecs.t5 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 8163 cores operating at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reflect its burstable CPU allocation and architectural constraints. Single-core performance is generally limited, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core benchmarks scoring in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Multi-core performance is relatively stronger, achieving average ratings in stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes fall within the average range, though database operations and memory scores in Passmark are categorized as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the burstable CPU model makes this instance a budget-friendly option for workloads with variable resource demands. It avoids the higher costs of dedicated-core instances by allowing users to pay for baseline performance with the ability to burst during peak periods. However, the lack of bundled local storage and poor single-threaded compression performance are notable tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, code compilation, and low-intensity background tasks rather than sustained high-performance database or heavy LLM inference workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 server configured with 8 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates weak single-core CPU and memory performance, though multi-core CPU metrics and memory bandwidth reach average tiers. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, the instance offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with fluctuating CPU demands. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks, software compilation, lightweight web serving, and development environments that do not require sustained, high-intensity compute resources.

tagline: A budget-friendly burstable server featuring eight Intel Xeon cores and sixteen gigabytes of memory for variable development and testing workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
